name: Signs of Assassins
description: Altaïr Commander deck-building workspace for Kyle's Assassin ledger, with owned-only and recommendation-enhanced variants.

variants.name: Hidden Blade Core
variants.description: Owned-only baseline build using Kyle's scanned batch, with no ghost slots.
variants.notes: Baseline comparison build. If it falls below 36 lands, keep the land-light flag visible instead of hiding missing basics.

variants.name: Rooftop Evasion
variants.description: Owned-only combat build prioritizing flying, menace, unblockable lines, and attacks that are awkward to block.
variants.notes: This variant tests the core feel of “you cannot block me” using only owned cards.

variants.name: Memory Corridor
variants.description: Graveyard/exile build with recommended setup pieces and ghost lands.
variants.notes: This variant loads the graveyard with useful Assassin bodies and uses exile/graveyard value without becoming a generic reanimator deck.

variants.name: Brotherhood Arsenal
variants.description: Equipment, artifact, and protection build with recommended support upgrades and ghost lands.
variants.notes: This variant asks whether the deck is better as a toolbelt: protect Altaïr, suit up evasive creatures, and let artifacts smooth the plan.

variants.name: Nothing Is True
variants.description: Stronger recommendation-enhanced build focused on fixing, efficient setup, protection, and clean interaction.
variants.notes: This is the cleanest “make the deck work” variant: stronger mana, setup, protection, and answers before more speculative packages.

variants.name: Everything Is Permitted
variants.description: Experimental puzzle/evasion build using the most playful recommendation package.
variants.notes: This variant is where the weird lines live: unblockable puzzles, attack-trigger multiplication, type manipulation, and temporary-token experiments.

notes: Imported owned cards are the source pool for this deck family, not a finalized 100. Ghost/recommended rows live in the Recommendations list and are visually marked as not owned in deck views. Owned-only variants intentionally keep missing land gaps visible.
